subject: ELA
title: Research & Writing Skills
description: Evaluating sources, essay structure, and writing conventions.

questions.q: Which source is MOST RELIABLE for a science report on climate change?
questions.explanation: NASA is a credible, expert scientific organization. Its articles are fact-checked.

questions.q: What is the PURPOSE of a THESIS STATEMENT?
options: State the main argument of an essay
questions.answer: State the main argument of an essay
questions.explanation: A thesis statement tells readers the central argument the essay will support.

questions.q: In an argumentative essay, what do you call the evidence that supports your claim?
questions.explanation: Evidence (facts, data, examples) is used to support a claim.

questions.q: Text that presents events in TIME ORDER uses which structure?
questions.explanation: Chronological order arranges events by time (first, next, then, finally).

options: Using someone else's words without giving credit
questions.answer: Using someone else's words without giving credit
questions.explanation: Plagiarism = copying or using another person's work without proper attribution.

questions.q: Which transition word BEST shows a contrast or opposing idea?
questions.explanation: 'However' signals that the next idea contrasts with the previous one.

questions.q: To PARAPHRASE means to restate something in your ___ words.
questions.explanation: Paraphrasing = reading something and restating it completely in your own words.

questions.q: Which is the BEST concluding sentence for a paragraph about recycling?
options: There are many types of plastic.
Clearly, recycling is one of the simplest ways we can protect our environment.
People throw away too much trash.
questions.answer: Clearly, recycling is one of the simplest ways we can protect our environment.
questions.explanation: A strong conclusion restates the main idea and gives it broader significance.

questions.q: In a well-organised paragraph, which comes FIRST?
questions.explanation: A paragraph begins with a topic sentence that states the main idea.

questions.q: What is the difference between a PRIMARY and SECONDARY source?
options: A primary source is original (e.g. diary, interview); secondary is a summary by someone else (e.g. textbook)
questions.answer: A primary source is original (e.g. diary, interview); secondary is a summary by someone else (e.g. textbook)
questions.explanation: Primary = original first-hand account. Secondary = someone else's interpretation or summary of primary sources.
